title: Automatic Handwritten Exam Paper Evaluation for Bangla and English
abstract: This research presents a computer vision-based automated evaluation system for handwritten examination scripts in both Bangla and English. The proposed framework integrates advanced image preprocessing, optical character recognition (OCR), and deep learning-based feature extraction to interpret handwritten responses. A hybrid grading mechanism combining semantic similarity and rule-based evaluation is employed to ensure consistent and scalable assessment. Experimental results demonstrate strong performance in text extraction and grading accuracy, highlighting its potential for deployment in large-scale academic environments.
dataset: Custom dataset of scanned handwritten exam scripts (Bangla & English)

supervisor.name: Professor Dr. S. M. Jahangir Alam
supervisor.organization: Bangladesh Army University of Science and Technology (BAUST)

coSupervisor.name: Saad Ahmed
coSupervisor.organization: Bangladesh Army University of Science and Technology (BAUST)

title: Tracking and Improved Correlation Analyses of Human Eye Movement
abstract: This study investigates human eye movement patterns using advanced tracking and optimized correlation analysis techniques. By leveraging computer vision-based gaze tracking and statistical modeling, the research improves fixation detection and behavioral interpretation. The enhanced correlation framework provides more robust insights into cognitive processes and attention dynamics.
dataset: Public eye-tracking datasets (e.g., MIT Gaze Dataset)

supervisor.name: Professor Dr. S. M. Jahangir Alam
supervisor.organization: Bangladesh Army University of Science and Technology (BAUST)

title: NLP-Based Spell Correction Using Word2Vec
abstract: This work proposes a context-aware spell correction system utilizing Word2Vec embeddings to capture semantic relationships between words. The system identifies and corrects misspelled words based on contextual similarity rather than dictionary lookup. Experimental results demonstrate improved correction accuracy in noisy and real-world text scenarios.

title: Dengue Prediction Using Ensemble Models
abstract: This research develops an ensemble-based machine learning framework for predicting dengue outbreaks using epidemiological and environmental data. The system combines multiple models through stacking and optimizes performance using ROC-AUC and threshold tuning. Results indicate improved predictive accuracy and robustness, supporting early warning systems.

title: Stroke Prediction: EDA, Resampling & XGBoost
abstract: This study presents a machine learning-based framework for early stroke prediction using clinical data. It incorporates exploratory data analysis, resampling techniques to address class imbalance, and XGBoost for classification. The model achieves improved sensitivity and predictive reliability for healthcare applications.

title: Bangla Offensive Words Identification and Blocking System
abstract: This research introduces an NLP-based system for detecting offensive Bangla text using embedding techniques and classification models. The system enables real-time filtering and demonstrates strong performance in low-resource language settings.

title: BChat – Integrated Social Media Communication Platform (Patent)
abstract: BChat is a proposed integrated platform combining messaging, digital services, and financial transactions within a unified ecosystem. The design emphasizes scalability, seamless interaction, and fintech integration tailored for Bangladesh.

supervisor.name: Professor Dr. S. M. Jahangir Alam
supervisor.organization: Bangladesh Army University of Science and Technology (BAUST)

title: Pretraining a Language Model from Scratch (BERT-like Model)
abstract: This project explores transformer-based language model pretraining from scratch, focusing on tokenization strategies, masked language modeling, and optimization techniques. It provides insights into building scalable NLP systems with limited resources.

title: Understanding Cross-Lingual Models
abstract: This research investigates multilingual transformer models to improve cross-lingual knowledge transfer. The study evaluates performance across low-resource languages and highlights the importance of multilingual representations in NLP.

title: AI-Based Image Processing for Social Good
abstract: This project applies computer vision and deep learning techniques to address real-world challenges in education, transportation, and public safety. The system focuses on building practical and impactful AI solutions.

title: Applied Machine Learning for Education Technology
abstract: This study explores machine learning applications in e-learning platforms, focusing on recommendation systems and adaptive learning. The system enhances personalization and improves student engagement.

title: B-Agent – A Smart Intelligent Agent
abstract: This project develops an intelligent agent capable of autonomous decision-making using reinforcement learning and AI planning techniques. The system demonstrates adaptive behavior in dynamic environments.

supervisor.name: Professor Dr. S. M. Jahangir Alam
supervisor.organization: Bangladesh Army University of Science and Technology (BAUST)

title: Screen Time, Sleep & Stress – EDA and Classification
abstract: This study analyzes the relationship between screen time, sleep patterns, and stress levels using exploratory data analysis and classification models. Results reveal strong behavioral correlations and predictive insights.

title: IoT-Enabled Smart Drainage Monitoring and Automated Water DischargeSystem with Dual Sensor False-Alarm Mitigation for Urban Areas in Bangladesh
abstract: This project proposes an IoT-based smart drainage monitoring system for urban flood prevention. Using real-time sensor data and automated alerts, the system enhances urban infrastructure resilience.
